tasks.title: Setup Development Infrastructure with Docker
tasks.description: Create docker-compose.yml for local development databases (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis) and LiveKit server configuration.
tasks.details:
Create docker-compose.yml at project root with services:
- PostgreSQL 16+ for Meta DB and sample Tenant DB (CQRS write)
- MongoDB 7+ for read-side queries
- Redis 7+ for JWT token blacklist and session management
- LiveKit server for WebRTC SFU

Configuration:
```yaml
services:
  postgres-meta:
    image: postgres:16
    ports: ['5432:5432']
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: meta_db
      POSTGRES_USER: admin
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: admin
    volumes:
      - postgres_meta_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
  
  postgres-tenant:
    image: postgres:16
    ports: ['5433:5432']
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: tenant_default
  
  mongodb:
    image: mongo:7
    ports: ['27017:27017']
  
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ['6379:6379']
  
  livekit:
    image: livekit/livekit-server:latest
    ports: ['7880:7880', '7881:7881']
    command: --dev --bind 0.0.0.0
```

Create .env.local template with connection strings.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `docker-compose up -d` and verify all containers start successfully. Test connectivity to each service using CLI tools (psql, mongosh, redis-cli). Verify LiveKit server responds on HTTP port.

subtasks.title: Create docker-compose.yml with PostgreSQL services
subtasks.description: Create docker-compose.yml file at project root with PostgreSQL 16 services for Meta DB (port 5432) and Tenant DB (port 5433) including volume mounts and environment configuration.
subtasks.details:
Create docker-compose.yml at project root with two PostgreSQL 16 services:

1. postgres-meta service:
   - Image: postgres:16
   - Container name: counseling-postgres-meta
   - Port mapping: 5432:5432
   - Environment: POSTGRES_DB=meta_db, POSTGRES_USER=admin, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=admin
   - Volume: postgres_meta_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   - Health check with pg_isready

2. postgres-tenant service:
   - Image: postgres:16
   - Container name: counseling-postgres-tenant
   - Port mapping: 5433:5432
   - Environment: POSTGRES_DB=tenant_default, POSTGRES_USER=admin, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=admin
   - Volume: postgres_tenant_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   - Health check with pg_isready

Define named volumes at the bottom of the file for data persistence. Use docker-compose version 3.8 format.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker-compose up -d postgres-meta postgres-tenant` and verify both containers start. Test connectivity with `psql -h localhost -p 5432 -U admin -d meta_db` and `psql -h localhost -p 5433 -U admin -d tenant_default`. Verify data persists across container restarts.

subtasks.title: Add MongoDB and Redis services to docker-compose.yml
subtasks.description: Extend docker-compose.yml with MongoDB 7 service for CQRS read-side queries and Redis 7-alpine service for JWT token blacklist and session management.
subtasks.details:
Add MongoDB and Redis services to the existing docker-compose.yml:

1. mongodb service:
   - Image: mongo:7
   - Container name: counseling-mongodb
   - Port mapping: 27017:27017
   - Environment: MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE=counseling_read
   - Volume: mongodb_data:/data/db
   - Health check using mongosh ping

2. redis service:
   - Image: redis:7-alpine
   - Container name: counseling-redis
   - Port mapping: 6379:6379
   - Command: redis-server --appendonly yes (for persistence)
   - Volume: redis_data:/data
   - Health check using redis-cli ping

Add corresponding named volumes (mongodb_data, redis_data) to the volumes section.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker-compose up -d mongodb redis` and verify containers start. Test MongoDB with `mongosh mongodb://localhost:27017/counseling_read`. Test Redis with `redis-cli -h localhost ping` expecting PONG response. Verify AOF persistence is enabled.

subtasks.title: Add LiveKit server service to docker-compose.yml
subtasks.description: Add LiveKit server service configuration to docker-compose.yml for WebRTC SFU functionality with development mode settings and proper port exposure.
subtasks.details:
Add LiveKit server service to docker-compose.yml:

1. livekit service:
   - Image: livekit/livekit-server:latest
   - Container name: counseling-livekit
   - Port mappings:
     - 7880:7880 (HTTP/WebSocket signaling)
     - 7881:7881 (RTC over TCP)
     - 7882:7882/udp (RTC over UDP)
   - Command: --dev --bind 0.0.0.0
   - Environment:
     - LIVEKIT_KEYS=devkey:secret (development API key)
   - Health check on HTTP port 7880

Note: In development mode (--dev flag), LiveKit uses simplified authentication suitable for local development. For production, proper key configuration would be required.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker-compose up -d livekit` and verify container starts. Test HTTP endpoint with `curl http://localhost:7880` expecting a response. Verify WebSocket is accessible. Check logs for successful startup with `docker-compose logs livekit`.

subtasks.title: Create .env.example with all connection strings and configuration
subtasks.description: Populate the existing .env.example file with all required environment variables including database connection strings, Redis URL, LiveKit configuration, and JWT settings.
subtasks.details:

# Database - PostgreSQL Meta DB
META_DB_HOST=localhost
META_DB_PORT=5432
META_DB_NAME=meta_db
META_DB_USER=admin
META_DB_PASSWORD=admin
META_DB_URL=r2dbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/meta_db

# Database - PostgreSQL Tenant DB (Default)
TENANT_DB_HOST=localhost
TENANT_DB_PORT=5433
TENANT_DB_NAME=tenant_default
TENANT_DB_USER=admin
TENANT_DB_PASSWORD=admin
TENANT_DB_URL=r2dbc:postgresql://localhost:5433/tenant_default

# MongoDB (Read-side CQRS)
MONGODB_URI=mongodb://localhost:27017/counseling_read

# Redis
REDIS_HOST=localhost
REDIS_PORT=6379
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# LiveKit
LIVEKIT_URL=ws://localhost:7880
LIVEKIT_API_KEY=devkey
LIVEKIT_API_SECRET=secret

# JWT
JWT_SECRET=your-jwt-secret-key-change-in-production
JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION=3600000
JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION=604800000

API_ADMIN_PORT=8081

FILE_STORAGE_PATH=./storage/files
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify .env.example contains all required variables. Copy to .env.local and verify Spring Boot application can read configuration. Test that connection strings match docker-compose service ports and credentials.

subtasks.title: Add docker-compose convenience scripts and documentation
subtasks.description: Create helper commands in docker-compose.yml using profiles, add docker-compose.override.yml.example for custom overrides, and ensure .gitignore properly excludes sensitive files.
subtasks.details:
Final configuration and convenience setup:

1. Update docker-compose.yml:
   - Add 'depends_on' with health check conditions between services
   - Add a default network 'counseling-network' for service communication
   - Add profiles for selective service startup (e.g., 'db', 'all')
   - Add restart policy: unless-stopped for all services

2. Verify .gitignore includes:
   - docker-compose.override.yml (already present)
   - .env and .env.* (except .env.example) (already present)

3. Add helpful labels and comments in docker-compose.yml:
   - Service descriptions
   - Port usage documentation
   - Commands for common operations in comments

4. Create storage directories structure:
   - storage/recordings (for LiveKit egress)
   - storage/files (for file uploads)
   - storage/captures (for screen captures)
   Add these to .gitignore with placeholder .gitkeep files.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker-compose up -d` to start all services. Verify health checks pass with `docker-compose ps`. Test `docker-compose down -v` removes volumes. Verify storage directories exist. Run `docker-compose config` to validate YAML syntax. Test profile-based startup with `docker-compose --profile db up -d`.

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts
plugins {
    kotlin("jvm") version "2.3.0"
    kotlin("plugin.spring") version "2.3.0"
    id("org.springframework.boot") version "4.0.2"
    id("io.spring.dependency-management") version "1.1.6"
    id("org.jlleitschuh.gradle.ktlint") version "12.1.2"

kotlin {
    jvmToolchain(25)
}

dependencies {
    implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-webflux")
    implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-data-r2dbc")
    implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-data-mongodb-reactive")
    implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-data-redis-reactive")
    implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-security")
    implementation("io.jsonwebtoken:jjwt-api:0.12.6")

    testImplementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-test")
    testImplementation("io.kotest:kotest-runner-junit5:5.9.1")
```

- application/ (use cases, ports)
- infrastructure/ (adapters, repositories)

subtasks.title: Create hexagonal architecture package structure with base classes
subtasks.description: Set up the hexagonal architecture package structure (domain, port, application, adapter) with placeholder classes and appropriate package-info documentation for each layer.
subtasks.details:
Create package structure under be/api/src/main/kotlin/com/counseling/api/:
- domain/ - Pure business entities and value objects (no framework dependencies)
- domain/model/ - Domain entities
- domain/event/ - Domain events
- port/ - Interface definitions
- port/inbound/ - Use case interfaces (input ports)
- port/outbound/ - Repository and external service interfaces (output ports)
- application/ - Service implementations that orchestrate domain and ports
- application/service/ - Service classes implementing use cases
- adapter/ - Framework-specific implementations
- adapter/inbound/web/ - REST controllers and DTOs
- adapter/outbound/persistence/ - JPA/R2DBC/MongoDB repository implementations
- adapter/outbound/external/ - External service adapters (LiveKit, email)
- config/ - Spring configuration classes
Note: Use 'inbound' instead of 'in' as 'in' is a Kotlin reserved keyword
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all packages are created with correct naming. Ensure no 'in' or 'out' package names exist (use 'inbound'/'outbound'). Run './gradlew compileKotlin' to ensure package structure is valid.

tasks.title: Implement Multi-Tenant Database Routing
tasks.description: Build dynamic database routing infrastructure for tenant isolation with Meta DB storing tenant connection info.
tasks.testStrategy: Write integration tests with multiple tenant contexts. Verify queries route to correct database. Test TenantWebFilter extracts tenant correctly from requests. Validate connection pool management under concurrent tenant access.

subtasks.title: Create Meta DB Schema and R2DBC Entity Configuration
subtasks.description: Define the PostgreSQL Meta DB schema for tenants table and super_admins table, and create corresponding R2DBC entity classes with Spring Data R2DBC repositories.
subtasks.details:
Create the Meta DB schema as SQL migration files:

1. Create SQL migration file `db/migration/meta/V1__create_meta_schema.sql`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE tenants (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    db_host VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    db_port INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 5432,
    db_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    db_username VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    db_password VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE super_admins (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_tenants_status ON tenants(status);
```

2. Create Kotlin entity classes in `domain/model/`:
- `Tenant.kt` - Entity with UUID id, name, status (enum ACTIVE/INACTIVE), dbHost, dbPort, dbName, dbUsername, dbPassword, timestamps
- `SuperAdmin.kt` - Entity with UUID id, username, passwordHash, timestamps
- `TenantStatus.kt` - Enum with ACTIVE, INACTIVE values

3. Create R2DBC repository interfaces in `port/outbound/`:
- `TenantRepository.kt` extending ReactiveCrudRepository with findByStatus() method
- `SuperAdminRepository.kt` extending ReactiveCrudRepository with findByUsername() method

4. Configure dedicated Meta DB ConnectionFactory bean for Meta DB operations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write integration tests using @DataR2dbcTest with embedded PostgreSQL or TestContainers. Test: (1) Tenant CRUD operations, (2) SuperAdmin findByUsername query, (3) Verify tenant status filtering works correctly. Use Kotest for pure unit tests validating entity validation logic.

subtasks.title: Implement TenantContext with Reactor Context Propagation
subtasks.description: Create TenantContext object for storing and retrieving tenant ID using Reactor's Context, enabling reactive tenant propagation throughout the request lifecycle.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests with Kotest verifying: (1) getCurrentTenant() returns default when no context set, (2) getCurrentTenant() returns correct tenant when context is set, (3) Context propagation through Mono/Flux chains works correctly, (4) TenantNotFoundException is thrown when tenant is required but missing.

subtasks.title: Implement TenantConnectionRegistry for Dynamic Connection Management
subtasks.description: Build TenantConnectionRegistry service that manages dynamic R2DBC ConnectionFactory instances for each tenant, with caching and lazy initialization support.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write integration tests with TestContainers running multiple PostgreSQL instances: (1) Verify ConnectionFactory is created and cached for valid tenant, (2) Test cache invalidation removes connection, (3) Test inactive tenant returns empty Mono, (4) Verify connection pool configuration is applied correctly, (5) Load test with concurrent access to verify thread safety of ConcurrentHashMap.

subtasks.title: Implement TenantRoutingConnectionFactory with AbstractRoutingConnectionFactory

subtasks.title: Implement QueueService with Redis Sorted Set Operations
subtasks.description: Build the core QueueService using ReactiveRedisTemplate with sorted set operations for queue ordering by entry time.
subtasks.details:
Create QueueService.kt in be/api/src/main/kotlin/service/queue/ directory:

1. Dependencies:
- ReactiveRedisTemplate<String, String> (for Redis operations)
- ObjectMapper (for JSON serialization)
- NotificationService (for WebSocket notifications - can be placeholder initially)

2. Redis Key Structure:
- Queue key: 'tenant:{tenantId}:counseling:queue' (tenant-specific)
- Score: entry timestamp in epoch milliseconds for FIFO ordering

3. Core Methods:
- enterQueue(tenantId: String, customer: CustomerInfo): Mono<QueueEntry>
  * Generate UUID, create QueueEntry with current timestamp
  * Add to sorted set with ZADD (score = enteredAt.toEpochMilli)
  * Trigger queue update notification
  * Return created entry

- leaveQueue(tenantId: String, entryId: UUID): Mono<Boolean>
  * Find entry by scanning queue (ZRANGE with pattern match)
  * Remove entry with ZREM
  * Trigger queue update notification
  * Return success boolean

- getQueueEntries(tenantId: String): Flux<QueueEntry>
  * Use ZRANGE to get all entries ordered by score (earliest first)
  * Deserialize JSON strings to QueueEntry objects

- getQueuePosition(tenantId: String, entryId: UUID): Mono<Int>
  * Use ZRANK to get 0-based position, add 1 for 1-based

- getQueueSize(tenantId: String): Mono<Long>
  * Use ZCARD to get total queue size

4. Configure Redis connection in application.yml with connection pool settings.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write integration tests using @SpringBootTest with embedded Redis (Testcontainers): 1) Test enterQueue adds entry and returns correct position, 2) Test leaveQueue removes entry correctly, 3) Test queue ordering maintains FIFO by entry time, 4) Test getQueuePosition returns correct 1-based position, 5) Test concurrent enter operations maintain consistency with 50+ simultaneous requests.

subtasks.title: Implement JWT Token Generation for LiveKit Access
subtasks.description: Add token generation methods to LiveKitService using LiveKit AccessToken API with proper grants for room join, publish, and subscribe permissions.
subtasks.details:
Extend LiveKitService with token generation capabilities:

1. Add token generation methods:
```kotlin
fun generateToken(
    roomName: String,
    participantId: String,
    participantName: String,
    grants: LiveKitGrants
): String {
    val token = AccessToken(liveKitProperties.apiKey, liveKitProperties.apiSecret)
    token.identity = participantId
    token.name = participantName
    token.addGrants(
        RoomJoin(true),
        RoomName(roomName),
        CanPublish(grants.canPublish),
        CanSubscribe(grants.canSubscribe),
        CanPublishData(grants.canPublishData)
    )
    token.ttl = Duration.ofHours(4)  // Token valid for 4 hours
    return token.toJwt()
}

fun generateAgentToken(roomName: String, agentId: UUID, agentName: String): String {
    return generateToken(roomName, agentId.toString(), agentName,
        LiveKitGrants(canPublish = true, canSubscribe = true, canPublishData = true))
}

fun generateCustomerToken(roomName: String, customerId: String, customerName: String): String {
    return generateToken(roomName, customerId, customerName,
        LiveKitGrants(canPublish = true, canSubscribe = true, canPublishData = true))
}
```

2. Create LiveKitGrants data class:
- canPublish: Boolean (camera/mic publish)
- canSubscribe: Boolean (receive streams)
- canPublishData: Boolean (data channel for chat)

3. Token configuration:
- Set appropriate TTL (4 hours default, configurable)
- Include metadata in token if needed (role, tenant)

4. Handle token expiration and refresh scenarios.

subtasks.title: Implement Feedback Management API and Admin Authentication Endpoints
subtasks.description: Create FeedbackController for viewing customer feedback with filtering/pagination, and implement AdminAuthController for admin-specific login, logout, and token refresh endpoints.
subtasks.details: Create FeedbackController.kt at /api-adm/feedbacks. Implement endpoints: GET /api-adm/feedbacks (list with filters: agentId, groupId, rating range, date range, pagination), GET /api-adm/feedbacks/{id} (feedback detail with channel/agent info). Create FeedbackQueryService reading from MongoDB FeedbackReadModel. Create AdminAuthController.kt at /api-adm/auth with endpoints: POST /api-adm/auth/login (authenticate admin/superuser, return JWT with role and tenantId claims), POST /api-adm/auth/logout (blacklist token via Redis), POST /api-adm/auth/refresh (refresh token pair). AdminAuthService distinguishes between SuperAdmin (from Meta DB) and tenant admins (from Tenant DB). SuperAdmin JWT contains no tenantId, while tenant admin JWT contains tenantId claim. Reuse JwtTokenService and TokenBlacklistService from Task 5 with admin-specific token configuration. Define DTOs: FeedbackListResponse, FeedbackDetailResponse, AdminLoginRequest, AdminLoginResponse with user role info.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write tests: (1) Feedback list filters by rating range correctly, (2) Feedback detail includes agent and channel information, (3) SuperAdmin login authenticates against Meta DB without tenantId, (4) Company/Group Admin login authenticates against Tenant DB with tenantId in JWT, (5) Logout blacklists admin token, (6) Refresh token generates new token pair with same claims, (7) GROUP_ADMIN sees only feedback for their group's agents.
